atoms.source_snippet: FSM parity chain: precompile address (0x080C / 0x0820) -> expected registry Capability for payload builder + verifier executor check -> traffic-gen ExecutorSelector capability -> ritual-node docker.py CAPABILITIES= on localnet HTTP sidecars.
atoms.semantic_claim: Sovereign Agent `0x080C` and Persistent Agent `0x0820` must map to `AsyncPrecompileCapabilityTag::HttpCall` (registry u8 0), matching traffic-gen `Capability.HTTP_CALL` executor selection and localnet HTTP executor provisioning — not `AutonomousAgent` / 11.

destinations.snippet: SOVEREIGN_AGENT_PRECOMPILE / PERSISTENT_AGENT_PRECOMPILE entries use HttpCall; regression test locks HTTP vs AutonomousAgent mismatch
destinations.evidence: Unit test `sovereign_and_persistent_precompiles_expected_capability_is_http_call_for_downstream_parity` fails if metadata maps agent precompiles to AutonomousAgent.

destinations.snippet: selector picks capability=Capability.HTTP_CALL for sovereign launch
destinations.evidence: launch_sovereign_agent uses ExecutorSelector with HTTP_CALL (lines ~460-470).
launch_persistent_agent pins HTTP_CALL executors (~508-518).
executor-go-http services pass capability=Capability.HTTP_CALL.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Run `cargo test -p ritual-precompile-addresses sovereign_and_persistent_precompiles_expected_capability_is_http_call_for_downstream_parity --locked`; on mismatch, `make launch-sovereign-agent` fails with HttpCall vs AutonomousAgent executor error.
atoms.notes: Seeded by incident: Reth accepted 0x080C as two-phase but expected AutonomousAgent while TEE registry executors are HTTP_CALL. Green Reth-only atoms are insufficient.

atoms.source_snippet: 0x0801 async precompile: Reth metadata tag, payload executor check, traffic Capability.HTTP_CALL, docker CAPABILITIES — verified by coverage-map/verify_async_precompile_cross_repo.py
atoms.semantic_claim: Cross-repo executor contract for 0x0801 (HTTP_CALL_PRECOMPILE): `AsyncPrecompileCapabilityTag::HttpCall` must match traffic-gen `Capability.HTTP_CALL` selection and ritual-node docker executor `Capability.HTTP_CALL` provisioning; payload/verifier compares via `registry_capability_for_precompile`.

destinations.evidence: Metadata entry binds 0x0801 to registry tag HttpCall (u8 0).
Payload/async settlement enforces TEE registry capability equals metadata-derived expectation.
Traffic-gen selects `Capability.HTTP_CALL` for short HTTP workloads referencing 0x0801.
Localnet executor services set CAPABILITIES consistent with `Capability` enum.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: python3 coverage-map/verify_async_precompile_cross_repo.py (must exit 0).
atoms.notes: Old-FSM parity uses downstream-selected executor capability, not name-plausible registry variants.

summary: Per-precompile cross-repo contract atoms are present; broad capability coverage is not accepted as proof of runtime parity.
notes: First-principles async audit: 0x080C/0x0820 map to HttpCall for old-FSM parity; media/FHE static capability atoms are capability-only and do not green-light runtime delivery/pricing.
